draft:
Dear Sir or Madam:

I am writing this letter to confirm some facts about Mr. Rohan Verma's work, as well as to provide my perspective on his abilities and accomplishments. Having worked closely with Mr. Verma on several projects, I have had the opportunity to observe his expertise and skills firsthand.

By way of introduction, I am an HCI Research Scientist with a robust background in human-computer interaction (HCI), software development, and user experience design. I hold a PhD in Interactive Technology from Espoo University, where I focused on eye tracking and gaze-based HCI for wearables such as AR/VR devices. My academic credentials also include a Master’s degree in Human Technology Interaction from the University of Espoo and a Bachelor’s degree in Electronics and Instrumentation from the University of Mysore.

Throughout my career, I have gained extensive experience in both academic and industry settings. I currently serve as a Research Scientist at Ascension, a company dedicated to advancing the science of agents and multi-agent systems. Prior to this, I was a Senior User Experience Designer at Visionix in Helsinki, Finland, where I developed and evaluated concepts for visually aware computing devices, contributing to patents and thought leadership in the field. I also held research positions at the University of Espoo, working within the Visual Interaction Research Group (VIRG), and completed internships at Hewlett-Packard and TCS, where I applied my skills to projects in smart education and network management.

My contributions to the field of HCI are reflected in my extensive publication record, with over 30 peer-reviewed articles involving both quantitative and qualitative research methods. I have also been involved in patenting innovative technologies related to eye-tracking and user interaction. My skills span a wide range of areas, including user experience design, programming, data analysis, and rapid prototyping, enabling me to bridge the gap between research and practical application.

I first became acquainted with Mr. Verma during our time at Visionix, where we collaborated on multiple user experience (UX) and eye-tracking projects. At Visionix, we worked together to explore novel HCI solutions, file patents, and conduct research in the areas of AR/VR. One of the projects we collaborated on was the "Digital Assistant and Method for Voice-Based Interactive Communication Based on Detected User Gaze Indicating Attention" (Patent Number: 35281726). This invention pertains to a method for voice-based interactive communication using a digital assistant, which includes steps like attention detection, speaker detection, speech sound detection (including lip movement detection), speech analysis, and providing feedback based on recorded speech. The patent was filed on December 21, 2021, and granted on July 14, 2024, with Visionix AB as the assignee. Our collaboration on this project allowed me to observe Mr. Verma's innovative thinking and expertise in user experience design and eye-tracking technology.

Through our collaboration, I had the opportunity to observe Mr. Verma's expertise and skills firsthand. As a UX Designer at Visionix Technologies from August 2018 to December 2020, Mr. Verma made significant contributions to the development of eye-tracking technology and its applications. One of his notable achievements was his work on dual-display devices, where he designed eye-tracking based interactions for products like AMD Prism and Lenovo ScreenTouch. These innovations enhanced user experience by enabling more intuitive and efficient interactions with dual-display setups. Additionally, Mr. Verma conducted extensive AR UX research, identifying opportunities in eye-tracking based AR interactions. He prototyped these concepts and successfully filed four patents, showcasing his ability to translate research insights into practical solutions. His contributions were recognized by Visionix, as he was awarded the title of top innovator for Q1 2020.

Furthermore, Mr. Verma explored smartphone eye-tracking based interactions, researching and conceptualizing designs that leveraged eye-tracking technology to improve smartphone usability. He also identified opportunities for integrating eye-tracking into visually aware voice assistants, designing new experiences and interactions that made these systems more responsive and user-friendly. Mr. Verma's work at Visionix Technologies demonstrated his ability to push the boundaries of what eye-tracking technology could achieve, and his contributions have had a lasting impact on the field.

Prior to his role at Visionix, Mr. Verma gained valuable experience as a Lead Designer at LG Electronics from June 2017 to March 2018. In this position, he worked on several innovative projects, including the design of LG's Athena voice assistant. Mr. Verma played a crucial role in defining the personality and UX principles of Athena, identifying major use-cases and collaborating with field experts to create an intuitive and efficient interaction design. His contributions were instrumental in the successful launch of Athena, which achieved 12 million daily active users and was integrated into all LG products. Additionally, Mr. Verma worked on the interaction paradigm of hearables, designing voice assistant-enabled wireless headphones based on user research. His designs were commercialized into LG's flagship device, the Halo Buds. He also contributed to the development of a sociable robot, focusing on its personality, out-of-box experience (OOBE), adaptiveness, and multi-user scenarios, and identified key scenarios for a foldable smartphone, providing UX guidelines for its development. Mr. Verma's work at LG Electronics demonstrated his ability to design cutting-edge user experiences and his capacity to contribute to large-scale, innovative projects.

In addition to his significant contributions at Visionix and LG, Mr. Verma has also made remarkable strides as an entrepreneur and innovator. In June 2024, he founded Soundly AI, a state-of-the-art AI content creation platform that allows users to create music and podcasts with ease. Soundly uses generative AI to help users brainstorm and translate their stories into musical reality, with the mission to democratize music creation for all aspiring creators. The platform has already gained significant traction, with over 1.2 million songs generated by more than 850,000 creators. Soundly AI has been featured in various media outlets, including Creators Now, Rhythm Nexus, Acoustic, and The Urban Tribune, highlighting its impact on the music industry and its potential to revolutionize content creation.

Before founding Soundly AI, Mr. Verma served as a Product Manager at Tidal from January 2021 to May 2024. In this role, he led product strategy, roadmap, vision, and execution within Tidal's music creation business unit, HarmonyHub. Working with a cross-functional team of engineers, designers, researchers, and data scientists, he built innovative music creation experiences. One of his notable achievements was transforming HarmonyHub into a real-time collaboration app, which led to a fourfold increase in studio collaboration time. He also crafted a product strategy to help Gen Z users achieve easier and contextual communication by building the "Chatter" feature, resulting in a 40% increase in user interaction with communication features. Mr. Verma's work at Tidal was widely recognized, with his contributions being featured in media outlets such as CrunchTech and Tidal's official YouTube channel.

Mr. Verma's diverse experiences, from leading design initiatives at major tech companies like LG and Visionix to founding an innovative AI platform, demonstrate his ability to drive technological advancements and create impactful user experiences. His work has consistently pushed the boundaries of what is possible in the fields of AI, AR, and music technology, and his contributions continue to shape the future of these industries.

In conclusion, Mr. Rohan Verma's extensive experience, innovative mindset, and proven track record of success make him a standout figure in the fields of AI, AR, and music technology. His ability to translate complex ideas into practical solutions, coupled with his passion for democratizing content creation, positions him as a leader who is poised to make significant contributions to the tech and creative industries. I am confident that Mr. Verma will continue to drive innovation and create transformative user experiences in his future endeavors.

If you require any additional information or clarification, please feel free to contact me at your convenience.

Sincerely,
Naveen Kapur, PhD
HCI Research Scientist
Ascension
naveen.kapur@asc.com
final:
Dear Sir or Madam:

I am pleased to offer this letter in recommendation of Mr. Rohan Verma, providing my perspective on his abilities and accomplishments. Having worked closely with Mr. Verma on several projects, I have had the opportunity to observe his expertise and skills firsthand.

As far as my own background, I am an HCI Research Scientist with a robust background in human-computer interaction (HCI), software development, and user experience design. I hold a PhD in Interactive Technology from Espoo University, where I focused on eye tracking and gaze-based HCI for wearables such as AR/VR devices. Throughout my career, I have gained extensive experience in both academic and industry settings. I currently serve as a Research Scientist at Ascension, a company dedicated to advancing the science of agents and multi-agent systems. Prior to this, I was a Senior User Experience Designer at Visionix in Helsinki, Finland, where I developed and evaluated concepts for visually aware computing devices, contributing to patents and thought leadership in the field. I also held research positions at the University of Espoo, working within the Visual Interaction Research Group (VIRG), and completed internships at Hewlett-Packard and TCS, where I applied my skills to projects in smart education and network management.

My contributions to the field of HCI are reflected in my extensive publication record, with over 30 peer-reviewed articles involving both quantitative and qualitative research methods. I have also been involved in patenting innovative technologies related to eye-tracking and user interaction. My skills span a wide range of areas, including user experience design, programming, data analysis, and rapid prototyping, enabling me to bridge the gap between research and practical application.

I first became acquainted with Mr. Verma during our time at Visionix, where we collaboratively engaged in several user experience (UX) and eye-tracking projects, notably contributing to the development of innovative HCI solutions and successfully filing patents. A significant highlight of our collaboration was the 'Digital Assistant and Method for Voice-Based Interactive Communication Based on Detected User Gaze Indicating Attention' (Patent Number: 35281726). This project demonstrated Mr. Verma's remarkable skills in integrating eye-tracking technology with voice-based interactive communication. His contributions were pivotal in refining methods for attention detection, speaker detection, speech sound detection—encompassing lip movement detection—and speech analysis, ultimately leading to enhanced feedback mechanisms based on recorded speech. This patent, filed on December 21, 2021, and granted on July 14, 2024, with Visionix AB as the assignee, stands as a testament to Mr. Verma's innovative thinking and profound expertise in user experience design and eye-tracking technology.

Moreover, Mr. Verma made significant contributions to the development of eye-tracking technology and its applications at Visionix. One of his notable achievements was his work on dual-display devices, where he designed eye-tracking based interactions for products like AMD Prism and Lenovo ScreenTouch. These innovations enhanced user experience by enabling more intuitive and efficient interactions with dual-display setups. Additionally, Mr. Verma conducted extensive augmented reality (AR) UX research, identifying opportunities in eye-tracking based AR interactions. He prototyped these concepts and successfully filed 4 patents, showcasing his ability to translate research insights into practical solutions. His contributions were recognized by Visionix, as he was awarded the title of top innovator.

Furthermore, Mr. Verma explored smartphone eye-tracking based interactions, researching and conceptualizing designs that leveraged eye-tracking technology to improve smartphone usability. He also identified opportunities for integrating eye-tracking into visually aware voice assistants, designing new experiences and interactions that made these systems more responsive and user-friendly. Mr. Verma's work at Visionix Technologies demonstrated his ability to push the boundaries of what eye-tracking technology could achieve, and his contributions have had a lasting impact on the field.

After his impactful tenure at Visionix, Mr. Verma served as a Product Manager at Tidal from January 2021 to May 2024. In this role, he was instrumental in leading product strategy, roadmap, vision, and execution within Tidal's music creation business unit, HarmonyHub. Collaborating with a cross-functional team of engineers, designers, researchers, and data scientists, he developed innovative music creation experiences. One of his notable achievements was transforming HarmonyHub into a real-time collaboration app, resulting in a fourfold increase in studio collaboration time. Additionally, he crafted a product strategy to help Gen Z users achieve easier and contextual communication by building the 'Chatter' feature, resulting in a 40% increase in user interaction with communication features. Mr. Verma's work at Tidal was widely recognized, with his contributions being featured in media outlets such as CrunchTech and Tidal's official YouTube channel.

In addition to his significant contributions at Visionix and LG, Mr. Verma has also made remarkable strides as an entrepreneur and innovator. In June 2024, he founded Soundly AI, a state-of-the-art AI content creation platform that allows users to create music and podcasts with ease. Soundly uses generative AI to help users brainstorm and translate their stories into musical reality, with the mission to democratize music creation for all aspiring creators. Under his leadership, the company was accepted into the prestigious Google for Startups - AI Scaleup Program, where Mr. Verma and Soundly AI were awarded $300k in Google Cloud credits, underscoring their potential for industry impact and growth. The platform has already gained significant traction, with over 1.2 million songs generated by more than 850,000 creators. Soundly AI has been featured in various media outlets, including Creators Now, Rhythm Nexus, Acoustic, and The Urban Tribune, highlighting its impact on the music industry and its potential to revolutionize content creation.

Mr. Verma's diverse experiences, from leading design initiatives at major tech companies like Tidal and Visionix to founding an innovative AI platform, demonstrate his ability to drive technological advancements and create impactful user experiences. His work has consistently pushed the boundaries of what is possible in the fields of AI, AR, and music technology, and his contributions continue to shape the future of these industries.

In summary, Mr. Rohan Verma's extensive experience, innovative mindset, and proven track record of success make him a standout figure in the fields of AI, AR, and music technology. His ability to translate complex ideas into practical solutions, coupled with his passion for democratizing content creation, positions him as a leader who is poised to make significant contributions to the tech and creative industries. I am confident that Mr. Verma will continue to drive innovation and create transformative user experiences in his future endeavors.

If you require any additional information or clarification, please feel free to contact me at your convenience.

Very truly yours,

Naveen Kapur, PhD
HCI Research Scientist
Ascension
